source.title: Accident Investigation Report - Hand Injury During AHU Belt Inspection

context.region: Gulf of America - Garden Banks Block 259

actions_taken: Injured worker reported to Rig Medic for treatment
Worker evacuated by medevac helicopter on 3 June 2025 to East Jefferson Hospital
Minor surgery performed to close wound
Worker released to work with light duty restrictions
BSEE conducted onsite Incident Follow-up Investigation on 4 June 2025
G-110 (C) Incident of Noncompliance (INC) issued to Hess Corporation
event.summary: On 02 June 2025, a Transocean Second Engineer sustained a hand injury during drilling operations at Garden Banks Block 259. While checking the tension on an air handling unit (AHU) belt in the Mud Module Electrical Room, he placed his left hand on a moving belt without proper lockout/tagout isolation. His hand was drawn between the belt and pulley, resulting in partial amputation of the left hand middle and ring fingertips. The engineer had shut off power but did not perform full energy isolation, worked without supervision, and did not communicate his task change to his supervisor. He was evacuated and treated for amputation of distal ends of middle and ring fingers with open non-displaced fractures.
recommendations: Hess must submit a letter of explanation within 14 days to the Lafayette District Well Operations Section Chief on the Incident of Non-Compliance along with plans to prevent future incidents of this nature

hazards.name: Rotating machinery - AHU belt and pulley

threats.name: Contact with moving belt while energized

controls.name: Lockout/Tagout (LOTO) Energy Isolation System
controls.lod_basis: Primary procedural control to isolate energy before maintenance

supporting_text: The Transocean Second Engineer did not isolate the energy to the AHU using the lockout/tagout system
The task of checking the tension on the AHU belt would have required a full energy isolation (LOTO) in place per the requirements of Transocean's Energy Isolation Process
Prior to checking the AHU belt, he did not communicate his change of plans to anyone about turning off power to the AHU equipment versus the required full isolation by lockout/tagout

controls.name: Physical Guard on AHU Belt
controls.barrier_role: Physical barrier to prevent contact with moving parts
controls.lod_basis: Primary physical barrier to prevent contact with rotating equipment

supporting_text: He shut off the power to the AHU and removed the guard over the AHU belt
When inspecting the tension of the AHU belt, he placed his left hand on the belt while the belt and pully were still in motion

controls.name: Supervisory Approval Process for Maintenance Tasks
controls.barrier_role: Ensure proper authorization and oversight for maintenance work
controls.lod_basis: Secondary oversight control to verify proper procedures are followed

supporting_text: would have required a supervisory approval process for the task
The Transocean Second Engineer had no supervision at the time of the injury, nor did he communicate his decision to check the belt's tension to his supervisor
No supervision. The Transocean Second Engineer had no supervision at the time of the injury, nor did he communicate his decision to check the belt's tension to his supervisor

controls.name: Lone Worker Communication Policy
controls.lod_basis: Secondary procedural control for lone worker safety

supporting_text: while alone in the MMER performing another task
nor did he communicate his decision to check the belt's tension to his supervisor as required by the Transocean policy for Lone Workers
Prior to checking the AHU belt, he did not communicate his change of plans to anyone

controls.name: Hazard Identification Prompt Card for Line of Fire
controls.barrier_role: Identify and mitigate line of fire hazards
controls.lod_basis: Primary hazard identification tool for task planning

supporting_text: The Transocean Second Engineer did not adhere to a Transocean Hazard Identification Prompt Card that was completed for identifying line of fire hazards where personnel could be caught by moving equipment

Inadequate hazard analysis. A Transocean Hazard Identification Prompt Card was completed for identifying line of fire hazards where personnel could be caught by moving equipment but was not followed by the Transocean Second Engineer

controls.name: Rig Medic and Onsite Medical Treatment
controls.barrier_role: Provide immediate medical response to injury
controls.lod_basis: Emergency response to mitigate injury consequences

supporting_text: The Transocean Second Engineer reported to the Rig Medic for treatment

controls.name: Medical Evacuation (Medevac) Capability
controls.barrier_role: Transport injured personnel to advanced medical care
controls.lod_basis: Emergency response to enable advanced medical treatment

supporting_text: On 3 June 2025, he was evacuated by a medevac helicopter to East Jefferson Hospital located in Metairie, Louisiana for a medical evaluation and further treatment

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
